questionText: You are building a Minecraft house. You have 3 chests in one room and 5 chests in another. How many chests total?
solutionSteps: Count first group: 3
Count second group: 5

questionText: At a playground you find 4 red apples under one tree and 7 green apples under another tree. How many apples did you find altogether?
solutionSteps: First group: 4 apples
Second group: 7 apples

questionText: You are collecting coins in a video game. You earn 5 coins every time you jump over a mushroom. You jump over 6 mushrooms. Skip-count by 5s to find your total coins.
solutionSteps: Six jumps × 5 coins each = 30 coins

questionText: A detective is counting footprints. She sees groups of 2 footprints (one pair of feet) left by 7 different animals. Skip-count by 2s to find the total footprints.
solutionSteps: Seven animals × 2 footprints each = 14

questionText: You want to plant flowers in your garden. You have 4 rows and each row has 6 flowers. How many flowers total?
solutionSteps: Identify groups: 4 rows
Items per group: 6 flowers
Multiply: 4 × 6 = 24

questionText: A chef is making cookie boxes. She puts 8 cookies in each box and makes 3 boxes. How many cookies did she bake in total?
solutionSteps: Number of boxes: 3
Cookies per box: 8
Multiply: 3 × 8 = 24

questionText: You're arranging LEGO pieces in a rectangle: 5 columns and 3 rows. How many pieces do you need?
solutionSteps: Rows: 3
Columns: 5
Multiply: 3 × 5 = 15

questionText: A Minecraft farm has 6 rows of wheat with 7 stalks in each row. If you draw it as an array, how many total stalks are there?
solutionSteps: Rows: 6
Stalks per row: 7
Multiply (array): 6 × 7 = 42

questionText: A Minecraft building has walls that are flat and each wall has 4 equal sides and 4 right angles. What shape is each wall?
solutionSteps: Count the sides: 4 equal sides
Check the corners: 4 right angles
A quadrilateral with 4 equal sides and right angles is a square

questionText: You are decorating cookies. One cookie has 4 sides where opposite sides are equal and parallel, but the sides are NOT all equal. What shape is that cookie?
solutionSteps: 4 sides: yes (quadrilateral)
Opposite sides equal: yes
All sides equal: no
It's a rectangle

questionText: A chef is decorating a cake border: red rose, blue rose, yellow rose, red rose, blue rose, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: Identify the repeating unit: red, blue, yellow
The pattern repeats every 3
After blue comes yellow

questionText: A playground fence has: post, rail, rail, post, rail, rail, post, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: Identify the repeating unit: post, rail, rail
The pattern repeats every 3 items
After post comes rail

questionText: You kick a soccer ball. Is this a push or a pull? Which direction will the ball go?
answerFormula: A push force in the direction you kicked
distractors: A pull force opposite your kick
solutionSteps: Your foot hits the ball (contact force)
Your foot pushes the ball forward
A kick is a push in the direction of the kick

questionText: A detective notices a heavy box moved across the floor. She asks: was it pushed or pulled? She sees a rope attached to the front of the box. What force moved it?
answerFormula: A pull force (the rope pulled the box forward)
solutionSteps: Look at the rope: it's attached to the front
Someone pulled the rope
The box was pulled forward

questionText: Ice → Water → Steam. What are these three states of matter?
solutionSteps: Ice: hard, fixed shape → solid
Water: flows, takes shape of container → liquid
Steam: spreads out, fills space → gas

questionText: A cook heats a frozen stick of butter. First it is hard and keeps its shape. Then it melts into a runny liquid. What change happened to the butter?
answerFormula: It changed from solid to liquid (melting)
distractors: It changed from liquid to gas
It changed from solid to gas directly
solutionSteps: Hard butter with a fixed shape = solid
Runny butter that pours = liquid
Adding heat melted the solid into a liquid

questionText: You mix sand and sugar in a bowl. This is a...
answerFormula: Mixture (you can still separate the sand and sugar)
solutionSteps: Sand and sugar don't change into something new
You can still see both materials
They can be separated — it's a mixture

questionText: A chef tosses a salad with lettuce, tomatoes, and croutons. Is this a mixture or a new substance?
answerFormula: A mixture — each ingredient keeps its own identity and can be separated
distractors: A new substance formed by combining ingredients
A solution because everything mixed together
solutionSteps: Lettuce is still lettuce
Tomatoes are still tomatoes
You can pick them apart — it's a mixture

questionText: You stir 2 spoons of sugar into warm lemonade and the sugar disappears. What happened?
answerFormula: The sugar dissolved to form a solution — it is still there, just spread out in the liquid
solutionSteps: The sugar breaks into tiny pieces too small to see
It spreads evenly through the lemonade
The result is a solution (sugar dissolved in water)

questionText: A detective is trying to figure out if a clear liquid is plain water or salt water. She heats the liquid until all the water evaporates. A white crust is left behind. What was in the liquid?
answerFormula: Salt — it was a salt-water solution and the salt stayed when the water evaporated
distractors: Sugar — water always leaves a crust
Nothing — the white crust is just from the heat
solutionSteps: A solution has a solute dissolved in a solvent
Evaporating the water leaves the dissolved substance behind
The white crust proves salt was dissolved in the water

questionText: You cut a pizza into 8 equal slices. You eat 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza did you eat?
solutionSteps: Total equal parts: 8 (denominator)
Parts eaten: 3 (numerator)
Fraction = 3/8

questionText: A Minecraft cake is divided into 6 equal pieces. Creeper eats 2 pieces. What fraction of the cake is left?
solutionSteps: Total pieces: 6
Eaten: 2
Left: 6 - 2 = 4
Fraction left: 4/6

questionText: You slide a book across a smooth table and it goes far. Then you slide it across a rough carpet and it stops quickly. Why does it stop faster on the carpet?
answerFormula: The rough carpet creates more friction, which slows the book down faster
distractors: The carpet is heavier so it pushes back
The book is attracted to the carpet magnetically
solutionSteps: Friction is a force between two surfaces
Rough surfaces create more friction than smooth surfaces
More friction = the book slows down faster

questionText: On a seesaw, you weigh 60 pounds and sit 4 feet from the center. Your friend weighs 40 pounds. How far from the center should your friend sit to balance the seesaw?
solutionSteps: Balance: your weight × your distance = friend's weight × friend's distance
d = 6 feet

questionText: You put 3 big rocks on one side of a balance scale and 5 small pebbles on the other side. The big rocks' side goes down. What does this tell you?
answerFormula: The 3 big rocks are heavier (weigh more) than the 5 small pebbles
distractors: The pebbles are heavier because there are more of them
Both sides weigh the same because they balanced
solutionSteps: The side that goes down is heavier
3 big rocks side went down
So the 3 big rocks weigh more than the 5 pebbles

questionText: You stand between the sun and a wall. Where does your shadow appear?
answerFormula: On the wall behind you (opposite side from the sun)
solutionSteps: Light travels in straight lines from the sun
Your body blocks the light
The blocked area on the wall behind you is your shadow

questionText: At noon the sun is directly overhead and your shadow is very short. In the late afternoon the sun is low on the horizon. Is your shadow now shorter or longer than at noon?
answerFormula: Longer — when the sun is low, shadows are long
distractors: Shorter — the lower the sun, the shorter the shadow
The same length — shadow length doesn't change
solutionSteps: The angle of the sun changes shadow length
Noon: sun is high → shadow is short
Afternoon: sun is low → shadow stretches out and is long

questionText: You draw a butterfly. If you fold it in half down the middle, both halves match perfectly. The butterfly has a line of symmetry. How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
solutionSteps: A square can be folded in half horizontally: 1 line
Folded vertically: 2 lines
Folded diagonally (corner to corner): 2 more lines
Total: 4 lines of symmetry

questionText: A Minecraft pixel-art heart shape is drawn on a grid. You draw a vertical line down the middle and both sides match exactly. Is the heart symmetrical?
answerFormula: Yes — the vertical line is a line of symmetry
distractors: Only if it also has a horizontal line of symmetry
solutionSteps: Fold along the vertical center line
Left half matches right half
This is a line of symmetry — the shape is symmetrical

questionText: You drop a small pebble and a cork into a bucket of water. The pebble sinks and the cork floats. Why?
answerFormula: The pebble is denser than water (sinks); the cork is less dense than water (floats)
distractors: The pebble sinks because it is smaller
The cork floats because it is round
solutionSteps: Dense objects are heavier for their size
If an object is denser than water, it sinks
If an object is less dense than water, it floats

questionText: A chef has a bottle of oil and a bowl of water. She pours the oil into the water. The oil floats on top. What does this tell you about oil's density compared to water?
answerFormula: Oil is less dense than water
distractors: Oil and water have the same density
solutionSteps: Oil floats on water
Floating means less dense than the liquid below it
Oil is less dense than water

questionText: You have 15 diamonds in Minecraft. You trade 6 diamonds with a villager. How many diamonds do you have left?
solutionSteps: Start with: 15 diamonds
Subtract traded: 15 - 6 = 9
You have 9 diamonds left

questionText: A chef baked 24 muffins. The students ate 17 of them at lunch. How many muffins are left?
solutionSteps: Total baked: 24
Eaten: 17

questionText: A chef has 18 cupcakes to place equally on 3 plates. How many cupcakes go on each plate?
solutionSteps: Total cupcakes: 18
Number of plates: 3
Divide: 18 ÷ 3 = 6

questionText: In Minecraft you mined 347 blocks. What digit is in the hundreds place?
solutionSteps: 347: ones place = 7
Tens place = 4
Hundreds place = 3

questionText: A nature detective counted 582 bird calls in one day. What is the value of the digit 5 in 582?
answerFormula: 500 (5 is in the hundreds place, so its value is 500)
solutionSteps: 582: ones = 2, tens = 8, hundreds = 5
The 5 is in the hundreds place
Its value is 5 × 100 = 500

questionText: A Minecraft explorer mined 473 blocks. Round 473 to the nearest hundred.
solutionSteps: Look at the tens digit of 473: it is 7
7 ≥ 5, so round up
473 rounded to nearest hundred = 500

questionText: A chef orders 248 strawberries. She wants to estimate by rounding to the nearest ten. What should she write on her order?
solutionSteps: Look at the ones digit of 248: it is 8
8 ≥ 5, so round up
248 rounded to nearest ten = 250

questionText: Your Minecraft adventure started at 2:15 PM and ended at 3:45 PM. How long did it last?
solutionSteps: Start: 2:15 PM
End: 3:45 PM
From 2:15 to 3:15 = 1 hour
From 3:15 to 3:45 = 30 minutes
Total: 1 hour 30 minutes

questionText: A detective starts staking out a suspect at 10:40 AM and finishes at 12:10 PM. How long was the stakeout?
solutionSteps: Start: 10:40 AM
End: 12:10 PM
From 10:40 to 11:40 = 1 hour
From 11:40 to 12:10 = 30 minutes
Total: 1 hour 30 minutes

questionText: Your LEGO baseplate is 6 squares wide and 4 squares tall. How many squares cover the whole plate?
solutionSteps: Width: 6 squares
Height: 4 squares
Area = 6 × 4 = 24 square units

questionText: A chef's cutting board is 5 tiles long and 3 tiles wide. How many square tiles cover the cutting board?
solutionSteps: Length: 5 tiles
Width: 3 tiles
Area = 5 × 3 = 15 square tiles

questionText: Your Minecraft garden is a rectangle: 8 blocks long and 5 blocks wide. How many fence blocks do you need to go all the way around?
solutionSteps: Two long sides: 8 + 8 = 16
Two short sides: 5 + 5 = 10
Perimeter = 16 + 10 = 26 blocks

questionText: A detective draws a square crime scene boundary. Each side is 9 meters long. How long is the total boundary?
solutionSteps: A square has 4 equal sides
Each side: 9 meters
Perimeter = 4 × 9 = 36 meters

questionText: A playground is divided into 5 equal zones. Each zone is what fraction of the whole playground?
solutionSteps: Total equal parts: 5 (denominator)
Each part represents: 1 (numerator)
One zone = 1/5 of the playground

questionText: A chef cuts a loaf of bread into 4 equal slices. What fraction does each slice represent? If you take 3 slices, what fraction is that?
solutionSteps: 4 equal parts → each part is 1/4
Taking 3 parts = 3 × (1/4) = 3/4

questionText: A bar graph shows favorite Minecraft biomes: Forest = 8 students, Desert = 5 students, Ocean = 7 students. How many more students prefer Forest than Desert?
solutionSteps: Forest: 8 students
Desert: 5 students
Difference: 8 - 5 = 3 more students prefer Forest

questionText: A pictograph shows cookies baked each day: Monday = 3 symbols, Tuesday = 5 symbols, Wednesday = 2 symbols. Each symbol = 4 cookies. How many cookies were baked on Tuesday?
solutionSteps: Tuesday symbols: 5
Each symbol = 4 cookies
Total: 5 × 4 = 20 cookies

questionText: You hold the north pole of one magnet near the north pole of another magnet. What happens?
solutionSteps: Like poles (N-N or S-S) repel each other
Opposite poles (N-S) attract each other
N near N → they push apart

questionText: A detective uses a magnet to search for hidden objects. She finds the magnet sticks to a nail but not to a coin. What can she conclude about the nail?
answerFormula: The nail is made of a magnetic material (like iron or steel)
distractors: The nail is heavier than the coin
The nail has a north pole on its tip
solutionSteps: Magnets attract certain metals like iron and steel
The magnet sticks to the nail → nail is magnetic material
The coin doesn't stick → the coin is not a magnetic metal

questionText: Complete the pattern: circle, square, triangle, circle, square, ___
solutionSteps: Identify the repeating unit: circle, square, triangle
It repeats every 3 shapes
After circle, square comes triangle

questionText: A chef arranges cookies on a tray in a pattern: chocolate, vanilla, chocolate, vanilla, ___. What flavor comes next?
solutionSteps: Repeating unit: chocolate, vanilla (AB pattern)
Repeats every 2
After vanilla comes chocolate

questionText: Look at this shape sequence: small circle, medium circle, large circle, small circle, medium circle, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: The shapes repeat: small, medium, large
This is an ABC repeating pattern based on size
After medium comes large

questionText: A Minecraft path uses blocks in this sequence: dirt, stone, stone, dirt, stone, stone, ___. What is the next block?
solutionSteps: Repeating unit: dirt, stone, stone (ABB pattern)
Repeats every 3 blocks
After stone, stone comes dirt

questionText: You're crafting 6 sets of armor in Minecraft. Each set needs 24 iron pieces. How many iron pieces total?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 6 × 24

questionText: A chef is preparing for a party. She needs 7 trays and each tray holds 35 appetizers. How many appetizers does she prepare in total?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 7 × 35

questionText: Your Minecraft farm has 23 rows of crops with 15 plants per row. How many plants total?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 23 × 15

questionText: A detective finds that a warehouse has 18 shelves with 32 boxes on each shelf. How many boxes are in the warehouse?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 18 × 32

questionText: A chef baked 96 cookies to package equally into boxes of 8. How many boxes can she fill?
solutionSteps: Divide: 96 ÷ 8
8 goes into 9 once (8), remainder 1
Bring down 6: 16 ÷ 8 = 2
Answer: 12 boxes

questionText: In Minecraft, you have 156 gold ingots to smelt into 6 blocks each. Wait — actually you want to divide 156 gold ingots equally among 6 chests. How many ingots per chest?
solutionSteps: Divide: 156 ÷ 6
6 goes into 15 twice (12), remainder 3
Bring down 6: 36 ÷ 6 = 6
Answer: 26 ingots per chest

questionText: You multiply 47 by 100. What is the result?
solutionSteps: Multiplying by 100 shifts digits two places left
The 4 moves to the thousands place, the 7 to the hundreds place

questionText: A recipe calls for 3,500 milligrams of salt. How many grams is that? (1 gram = 1,000 milligrams, so divide by 1,000)
solutionSteps: Dividing by 1,000 shifts digits three places right
3,500 mg = 3.5 grams

questionText: Which numbers are factors of 24? Circle all that apply: 3, 5, 6, 7, 8
List the first 5 multiples of 7.
solutionSteps: Multiply 7 × 1 = 7

questionText: Find a fraction equivalent to 3/4 that has a denominator of 12.
solutionSteps: Multiply both numerator and denominator by the same number
4 × ? = 12 → multiply by 3

questionText: Which fraction is larger: 3/5 or 2/3?
solutionSteps: Find a common denominator: 15

questionText: A Minecraft trader offers you 5/8 of a diamond chest or 3/4 of a diamond chest. Which is the better deal?
answerFormula: 3/4 is more (= 6/8), so 3/4 of the chest is the better deal
solutionSteps: Convert to same denominator: 3/4 = 6/8
Compare: 5/8 vs 6/8

questionText: You hiked 2/8 of a trail before lunch and 3/8 of the trail after lunch. How much of the trail did you hike in total?
solutionSteps: Same denominators: 8
Add numerators: 2 + 3 = 5
Total: 5/8

questionText: A chef uses 1/6 cup of vanilla and 3/6 cup of milk in a recipe. How much liquid total?
solutionSteps: Same denominator: 6
Add numerators: 1 + 3 = 4
Total: 4/6 = 2/3 cup

questionText: Write 0.47 in words and as a fraction.
solutionSteps: The digit 4 is in the tenths place
The digit 7 is in the hundredths place
0.47 = 47 hundredths = 47/100

questionText: A nature trail is 1.35 kilometers long. Which decimal represents the same distance as 1 and 35 hundredths?
solutionSteps: Ones place: 1
Tenths place: 3
Hundredths place: 5
Written as: 1.35

questionText: A Minecraft fence is 5 yards long. How many feet is that? (1 yard = 3 feet)
solutionSteps: 1 yard = 3 feet
5 yards × 3 feet/yard = 15 feet

questionText: A chef's recipe says to cook for 2.5 hours. How many minutes is that? (1 hour = 60 minutes)
solutionSteps: 1 hour = 60 minutes
2.5 hours × 60 minutes/hour = 150 minutes

questionText: A Minecraft building floor plan looks like an L-shape. The big rectangle is 8 × 6 blocks and the corner cut-out is 3 × 2 blocks. What is the area of the floor?
solutionSteps: Area of big rectangle: 8 × 6 = 48
Area of cut-out: 3 × 2 = 6
L-shape area = 48 - 6 = 42 square blocks

questionText: A playground is shaped like two rectangles joined together: one is 5 × 4 meters and the other is 3 × 2 meters. What is the total area?
solutionSteps: Rectangle 1: 5 × 4 = 20 sq m
Rectangle 2: 3 × 2 = 6 sq m
Total: 20 + 6 = 26 sq m

questionText: A clock's hands form a right angle at a certain time. What does a right angle measure?
solutionSteps: A right angle is a square corner
It measures exactly 90°
A clock shows a right angle at 3:00 or 9:00

questionText: Two Minecraft pathways cross each other and form parallel lines on either side. What do we call lines that never cross and stay the same distance apart?
solutionSteps: Parallel lines never meet
They stay the same distance apart
Train tracks and opposite walls are examples

questionText: You're building fences in Minecraft. Each section needs n posts. If n = 7, how many posts for 4 sections?
solutionSteps: Expression: 4 × n
Substitute n = 7
4 × 7 = 28 posts

questionText: A detective earns d dollars per solved case. She solved 5 cases this month. Write an expression for her earnings and find the total if d = 20.
solutionSteps: Earnings = 5 × d
Substitute d = 20
5 × 20 = 100 dollars

questionText: In a recipe, you need 3 × x cups of flour. If you used 18 cups, what was x?
solutionSteps: Equation: 3x = 18
Divide both sides by 3

questionText: A Minecraft explorer found some diamonds. After giving away 9, she has 13 left. Write and solve an equation to find how many she started with.
solutionSteps: Let x = starting diamonds
x - 9 = 13 (after giving away 9)
Add 9 to both sides: x = 22

questionText: At the Minecraft market, each diamond costs 8 gold coins. You buy 9 diamonds. How much do you spend?
solutionSteps: Cost formula: Total = price × quantity
Total = 8 × 9
Total = 72 gold coins

questionText: A chef buys ingredients for $6 per bag. She needs 7 bags. What is her total cost?
solutionSteps: Total cost = price per bag × number of bags
Total = $6 × 7
Total = $42

questionText: Look at this pattern: 3, 6, 12, 24, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: Rule: multiply by 2 each time

questionText: A nature trail marker shows: mile 5, mile 9, mile 13, mile 17, ___. What is the next marker?
solutionSteps: Rule: add 4 each time

questionText: A ball rolling down a hill has which type of energy?
solutionSteps: Kinetic energy = energy of moving objects
The ball is rolling (moving)
Moving ball has kinetic energy

questionText: A Minecraft arrow is pulled back on a bow and held still before release. What type of energy does the stretched bow have?
answerFormula: Elastic potential energy (stored energy due to the stretched bow)
solutionSteps: The bow is stretched and not moving
Stored energy ready to be released = potential energy
Specifically elastic potential energy (from stretching)

questionText: When you pluck a guitar string and it makes a sound, what is vibrating?
solutionSteps: Sound is caused by vibrations
You plucked the string → string moves back and forth
The vibrating string makes the sound

questionText: You tap a glass of water with a spoon and it makes a ringing sound. If you put more water in the glass, the pitch gets lower. Why?
answerFormula: More water means slower vibrations (lower frequency), producing a lower pitch
distractors: More water absorbs the sound completely
More water means faster vibrations and higher pitch
solutionSteps: Vibrating glass creates sound waves
More water = more mass = slower vibrations
Slower vibrations = lower frequency = lower pitch

questionText: When you look in a mirror, you see your face because light...
answerFormula: Reflects off the mirror and into your eyes
solutionSteps: Light from a lamp bounces off your face
That light hits the mirror
The mirror reflects it into your eyes — you see your reflection

questionText: You put a straw in a glass of water and it looks bent. This is because light...
answerFormula: Refracts (bends) when it passes from water into air
distractors: Reflects off the water surface making it look bent
Is absorbed by the water and the image is distorted
solutionSteps: Light bends when it moves from one medium to another
Water to air: light changes speed and bends
This bending is called refraction — the straw appears bent

questionText: You drop a ball from a height. As it falls, potential energy converts to...
solutionSteps: At the top: maximum potential energy, minimum kinetic energy
As it falls: potential energy decreases, kinetic energy increases
Just before hitting the ground: maximum kinetic energy

questionText: In a Minecraft torch, chemical energy in the coal converts to what forms of energy?
solutionSteps: Burning coal releases stored chemical energy
The flame produces light we can see
The flame also produces heat we can feel

questionText: You stir salt into warm water and it disappears. What happened?
answerFormula: The salt dissolved to form a solution
solutionSteps: Salt breaks into tiny particles
Particles spread evenly through the water
The result is a homogeneous mixture called a solution

questionText: Why does sugar dissolve faster in hot tea than in iced tea?
answerFormula: Higher temperature increases the rate of dissolving — hot water molecules move faster and break apart the sugar more quickly
distractors: Cold water is denser so it blocks dissolving
Temperature has no effect on dissolving rate
solutionSteps: Hot water has more energy → molecules move faster
Faster-moving molecules collide with sugar more often
More collisions = sugar dissolves faster

questionText: Which property makes steel a good material for building bridges?
answerFormula: It is strong and doesn't bend easily (high strength)
solutionSteps: Bridges need to hold heavy loads
Strong materials resist bending and breaking
Steel is very strong → good for bridges

questionText: A chef wants to choose a pan that conducts heat well. Should she choose a wooden pan or a metal pan?
answerFormula: Metal pan — metals are good conductors of heat
solutionSteps: Conductivity: how well a material transfers heat
Metals are good thermal conductors
Wood is a poor heat conductor (insulator)
Metal pan is best for cooking

questionText: When water freezes, it changes from...
solutionSteps: Freezing = removing heat from a liquid
As water cools below 0°C it becomes ice
Ice is solid → water changed from liquid to solid

questionText: A chef boils a pot of water and notices steam rising. What state change is happening?
answerFormula: Evaporation/boiling — water (liquid) is changing to steam (gas)
distractors: Condensation — gas is turning to liquid
Melting — solid is turning to liquid
solutionSteps: Boiling = adding heat to a liquid
Water reaches 100°C and turns to steam
Steam is a gas — liquid changed to gas (evaporation/boiling)

questionText: Find the rule and next term: 2, 4, 8, 16, ___
solutionSteps: Rule: multiply by 2 each time

questionText: A Minecraft tower is built by adding floors: 50, 45, 40, 35, ___. The tower is losing floors somehow. What is next?
solutionSteps: Rule: subtract 5 each time

questionText: Sort these shapes into a Venn diagram. Left circle = has 4 sides. Right circle = has all equal sides. Where does a square go?
answerFormula: In the overlapping middle — a square has 4 sides AND all equal sides
distractors: Only in the left circle (just 4 sides)
solutionSteps: A square: 4 sides? Yes. All equal sides? Yes.
Both conditions are true
It belongs in the overlapping (middle) region

questionText: A detective classifies suspects: Attribute A = owns a cat, Attribute B = works at night. Where is someone who works at night but has no cat?
answerFormula: In the right circle only (Attribute B only — works at night, no cat)
solutionSteps: Check Attribute A (owns a cat): No → not in left circle
Check Attribute B (works at night): Yes → in right circle
Right circle only

questionText: If it rains, then the playground gets wet. It rained today. What do you know for sure?
solutionSteps: Given: If rain → playground wet (rule)
Given: It rained (condition is true)
Conclusion: The playground is wet (must follow)

questionText: You can choose a shirt (red or blue) and pants (black or white). How many different outfits can you make?
solutionSteps: Red shirt branches: red+black, red+white (2 outfits)
Blue shirt branches: blue+black, blue+white (2 outfits)
Total: 2 + 2 = 4 outfits
Multiply first: 4 × 2 = 8
Then add: 3 + 8 = 11
Then subtract: 11 - 1 = 10

questionText: In Minecraft, calculate your score: (5 + 3) × 2 - 4 ÷ 2. What is the result?
solutionSteps: Parentheses first: 5 + 3 = 8
Multiply: 8 × 2 = 16
Divide: 4 ÷ 2 = 2
Subtract: 16 - 2 = 14

questionText: You ran 1/3 of a mile before school and 1/2 mile after school. How far did you run total?
solutionSteps: Find common denominator: LCD(3, 2) = 6

questionText: A recipe needs 2/3 cup of oats and 3/4 cup of flour. How much dry ingredient is there total?
A recipe needs 3/4 cup of sugar. If you make 1/2 the recipe, how much sugar do you need?
solutionSteps: Multiply fractions: 1/2 × 3/4
Multiply numerators: 1 × 3 = 3
Multiply denominators: 2 × 4 = 8
Result: 3/8 cup

questionText: A Minecraft farm is 2/3 of a chunk wide and 3/5 of a chunk long. What is its area?
solutionSteps: Area = length × width
Simplify: 6/15 = 2/5

questionText: You have 3 cups of trail mix to divide equally into bags that each hold 1/2 cup. How many bags can you fill?
solutionSteps: Divide: 3 ÷ 1/2
Flip the fraction and multiply: 3 × 2/1
3 × 2 = 6 bags

questionText: A chef has 1/2 a pizza and wants to divide it into pieces that are each 1/8 of a whole pizza. How many pieces can she cut?
solutionSteps: Divide: 1/2 ÷ 1/8
Flip and multiply: 1/2 × 8/1
8/2 = 4 pieces

questionText: You have $12.75 and spend $4.30 on a snack. How much do you have left?
solutionSteps: Align decimal points: 12.75 - 4.30
Subtract ones: 5 - 0 = 5 hundredths
Subtract tenths: 7 - 3 = 4 tenths
Subtract ones: 12 - 4 = 8
Result: $8.45

questionText: A chef multiplies 2.4 kg of flour by 1.5 to scale up a recipe. How much flour does she need?
solutionSteps: Multiply ignoring decimals: 24 × 15 = 360
Count decimal places: 1 + 1 = 2 total
Place decimal: 3.60 = 3.6 kg

questionText: Your Minecraft treasure chest is 4 blocks long, 3 blocks wide, and 2 blocks tall. What is its volume?
solutionSteps: Volume = length × width × height
V = 24 cubic blocks

questionText: A baking pan is 9 inches long, 6 inches wide, and 2 inches deep. What is its volume?
solutionSteps: Volume = l × w × h
V = 108 cubic inches

questionText: In Minecraft's top-down map, you walk from point (2, 3) to point (5, 3). What direction did you move and how far?
answerFormula: 3 units to the right (in the positive x-direction)
solutionSteps: Starting point: (2, 3)
Ending point: (5, 3)
Y-coordinate unchanged → horizontal move
5 - 2 = 3 units to the right

questionText: A detective maps a city block. She marks Point A at (1, 2) and Point B at (4, 6). What are the coordinates of Point B?
answerFormula: x = 4, y = 6 — written as the ordered pair (4, 6)
solutionSteps: In coordinate notation, always write (x, y)
Move 4 units right from origin: x = 4
Move 6 units up from origin: y = 6
Point B = (4, 6)

questionText: A Minecraft minecart travels at 8 blocks per second. How far does it go in 6 seconds?
solutionSteps: Formula: d = v × t
d = 48 blocks

questionText: A detective chases a suspect at 6 km/h for 2.5 hours. How far does she travel?
solutionSteps: d = v × t
d = 6 km/h × 2.5 h
d = 15 km

questionText: You buy 3 packs of cards at $4.50 each, then sell 2 of them for $6 each. How much profit or loss did you make?
solutionSteps: Total spent: 3 × $4.50 = $13.50
Total earned: 2 × $6 = $12.00

questionText: A chef makes 5 pies, each requiring 2.5 cups of apples. She has 10 cups. Does she have enough? If not, how many more cups does she need?
answerFormula: No — she needs 12.5 cups but only has 10, so she needs 2.5 more cups
solutionSteps: Total needed: 5 × 2.5 = 12.5 cups
Amount she has: 10 cups
12.5 - 10 = 2.5 more cups needed

questionText: You drop a heavy textbook and a pencil from the same height at the same time. Which hits the ground first (ignoring air resistance)?
answerFormula: They hit at the same time (gravity accelerates all objects equally)
solutionSteps: Gravity pulls all objects toward Earth at the same rate
Acceleration due to gravity ≈ 9.8 m/s² regardless of mass
Both objects hit the ground at the same time

questionText: Why does a ball thrown straight up eventually come back down?
answerFormula: Gravity continuously pulls the ball downward, slowing it, stopping it, then pulling it back to Earth
distractors: Air resistance pushes it back down
The ball runs out of energy and falls
solutionSteps: Gravity is always pulling downward
As the ball moves up, gravity decelerates it
At the top, speed = 0; gravity then pulls it back down

questionText: A ramp helps you push a heavy box into a truck. How does the ramp help?
answerFormula: It spreads the work over a longer distance, so you need less force
distractors: It creates energy to lift the box
solutionSteps: Work = force × distance
Total work needed stays the same
Longer distance → smaller force needed to do the same work

questionText: You roll a ball down a hill and it slows to a stop at the bottom. Where did the kinetic energy go?
answerFormula: It converted to heat and sound energy due to friction with the ground
distractors: The energy was stored as potential energy again
solutionSteps: Energy cannot be created or destroyed (conservation of energy)
The rolling ball had kinetic energy
Friction between ball and ground converts kinetic energy to heat and sound

questionText: In Minecraft, a skeleton fires an arrow. As the arrow flies through the air, what energy transformation happens?
answerFormula: Elastic potential energy (stretched bow) → kinetic energy of the flying arrow
solutionSteps: Bow is drawn back: elastic potential energy stored
Arrow released: potential energy converts to kinetic energy
Total energy is conserved (some lost to sound/heat)

questionText: You fold a piece of paper in half. Is this a chemical or physical change?
answerFormula: Physical change — same substance, just different shape
distractors: Chemical change — paper becomes something new
solutionSteps: A physical change alters shape/size but not the substance
You can unfold the paper — it's still paper
No new substance was formed → physical change

questionText: A detective burns a piece of evidence (paper). After burning, there is only ash and smoke. Is this a chemical or physical change? How do you know?
answerFormula: Chemical change — new substances (ash, smoke, CO₂) were formed that cannot easily be turned back into paper
distractors: Physical change — the paper just changed shape
Neither — burning is not a change
solutionSteps: New substances formed: ash, smoke, gases
Cannot reverse it back to paper
Irreversible reaction forming new substances = chemical change

questionText: Oil floats on water because...
answerFormula: Oil is less dense than water
solutionSteps: Density = mass ÷ volume
Oil density ≈ 0.9 g/cm³; water density = 1.0 g/cm³
Oil is less dense → it floats on top of water

questionText: A block has a mass of 50 grams and a volume of 10 cm³. What is its density? Will it float or sink in water (density = 1 g/cm³)?
answerFormula: Density = 5 g/cm³; it will sink (denser than water)
solutionSteps: Density = mass ÷ volume = 50 ÷ 10 = 5 g/cm³
Compare to water: 5 > 1
Denser than water → it sinks

questionText: You dissolve 5g of sugar into 100g of water. What is the total mass of the solution?
solutionSteps: Law of conservation of mass: matter is not created or destroyed
Sugar dissolves but doesn't disappear
Total mass = 100g + 5g = 105g

questionText: A detective burns a 10g piece of wood. The ash weighs only 2g. What happened to the missing 8g of mass?
answerFormula: The 8g escaped as gases (carbon dioxide and water vapor) — total mass is still conserved
distractors: The mass was destroyed in the fire
The mass turned into heat energy and disappeared
solutionSteps: Conservation of mass: total mass before = total mass after
Wood (10g) → ash (2g) + gases (8g)
The gases escaped into the air; total is still 10g

questionText: A staircase pattern: 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: Differences grow by 1 each time
Next difference: 6; 15 + 6 = 21

questionText: A Minecraft pyramid uses layers: layer 1 = 1 block, layer 2 = 4 blocks, layer 3 = 9 blocks, layer 4 = 16 blocks. How many blocks does layer 5 need?
answerFormula: 25 blocks (each layer n has n² blocks)
solutionSteps: Layer 1: 1² = 1
Layer 2: 2² = 4
Layer 3: 3² = 9
Layer 4: 4² = 16
Layer 5: 5² = 25

questionText: Square is to cube as circle is to ___
solutionSteps: A square (2D) extended into 3D = cube
Apply same rule: circle (2D) extended into 3D
A circle in 3D = sphere

questionText: Hot is to cold as day is to ___
solutionSteps: Hot and cold are opposites
Apply the same relationship: day and ___
Day's opposite = night

questionText: All cats are mammals. All mammals are animals. Therefore...
distractors: Some cats might not be animals
solutionSteps: Premise 1: All cats → mammals
Premise 2: All mammals → animals
Chain: cats → mammals → animals
Conclusion: All cats are animals

questionText: All Minecraft diamonds are gemstones. All gemstones are valuable. Alex has a diamond. What can you conclude?
solutionSteps: Premise 1: All diamonds → gemstones
Premise 2: All gemstones → valuable
Alex has a diamond → Alex has a gemstone → Alex has something valuable

questionText: In your class, there are 12 boys and 8 girls. What is the ratio of boys to girls?
solutionSteps: Ratio of boys to girls: 12 to 8
Simplify by dividing both by 4

questionText: A Minecraft recipe for green dye uses 2 cacti for every 5 glass bottles. Write this ratio three ways.
solutionSteps: Ratio: 2 cacti for 5 glass bottles
Written as a colon: 2:5
Written as a fraction: 2/5
Written in words: 2 to 5

questionText: You can build 15 Minecraft blocks in 3 minutes. How many blocks can you build in 1 minute?
solutionSteps: Unit rate = total ÷ time
15 blocks ÷ 3 minutes = 5
5 blocks per minute

questionText: A chef makes 36 cupcakes in 4 hours. What is her unit rate (cupcakes per hour)?
solutionSteps: Unit rate = total amount ÷ time
She makes 9 cupcakes per hour

questionText: A recipe for 4 people needs 2 cups of rice. How much rice for 10 people?
solutionSteps: Set up proportion: 2/4 = x/10
Cross-multiply: 4x = 20
x = 5 cups

questionText: A Minecraft map uses a scale of 1 cm = 50 blocks. If two villages are 7 cm apart on the map, how far apart are they in blocks?
solutionSteps: Scale: 1 cm = 50 blocks
Set up proportion: 1/50 = 7/x
x = 7 × 50 = 350 blocks

questionText: You got 18 out of 20 questions right on a quiz. What percent did you get correct?
solutionSteps: Percent = (part/whole) × 100

questionText: A Minecraft trader has 60 emeralds and spends 15% on trades. How many emeralds does he spend?
solutionSteps: Find 15% of 60
0.15 × 60 = 9 emeralds

questionText: The temperature was 5°C. It dropped 8 degrees overnight. What is the temperature now?
solutionSteps: Start at 5°C on a number line
Drop 8 degrees: move 8 to the left

questionText: A submarine is at -40 meters (below sea level). It rises 25 meters. What is its new depth?
solutionSteps: Start at -40 meters
Rise 25 meters: -40 + 25

questionText: Calculate: (-3) × (-4)
solutionSteps: Rule: negative × negative = positive
Multiply absolute values: 3 × 4 = 12
Apply sign rule: positive → answer is 12

questionText: A Minecraft player loses 5 hearts per second for 3 seconds. Calculate the total change in health points using integers.
solutionSteps: Losing = negative change
Change per second: -5
Over 3 seconds: (-5) × 3 = -15
Lost 15 health points

questionText: Write an expression for: 3 more than twice a number n
solutionSteps: Twice a number n = 2n
3 more than that = 2n + 3

questionText: Evaluate the expression 4x - 7 when x = 5.
solutionSteps: Substitute x = 5: 4(5) - 7
Multiply: 20 - 7
Subtract: 13

questionText: Solve: x - 7 = 15
solutionSteps: Add 7 to both sides to isolate x

questionText: A Minecraft enchantment costs some XP. After paying it, you have 18 XP left from your original 30 XP. Solve for how much the enchantment cost: 30 - c = 18.
solutionSteps: Subtract 30 from both sides: -c = -12
Multiply both sides by -1: c = 12

questionText: Test scores: 85, 90, 78, 92, 85. What is the median?
solutionSteps: Sort the scores: 78, 85, 85, 90, 92
Find the middle value (3rd of 5)
Median = 85

questionText: A chef records her daily baking times (minutes): 45, 60, 45, 75, 55. Find the mean (average) baking time.
solutionSteps: Count: 5 values
Mean = 280 ÷ 5 = 56 minutes

questionText: A Minecraft gift box is a rectangular prism 5 blocks long, 4 blocks wide, and 3 blocks tall. What is its surface area?
solutionSteps: SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)

questionText: A chef wraps a rectangular cake box: 10 cm × 8 cm × 4 cm. How much wrapping paper does she need (surface area)?
solutionSteps: SA = 2(lw + lh + wh)

questionText: A minecart travels 120 blocks in 15 seconds. What is its speed?
solutionSteps: Speed = distance ÷ time
Speed = 120 ÷ 15
Speed = 8 blocks per second

questionText: A detective drives north at 60 km/h for 2 hours. What is the distance traveled? What is different about 'velocity' versus 'speed' in this problem?
answerFormula: Distance = 120 km. Velocity includes direction (60 km/h north); speed is just the number (60 km/h)
distractors: Distance = 62 km; they are the same thing
Distance = 30 km; velocity is faster than speed
solutionSteps: Distance = speed × time = 60 × 2 = 120 km
Speed = 60 km/h (magnitude only)
Velocity = 60 km/h north (magnitude + direction)

questionText: A shopping cart is hard to start moving and hard to stop. This is an example of Newton's...
answerFormula: First Law (inertia — objects resist changes in motion)
solutionSteps: Newton's 1st Law: objects resist changes in motion (inertia)
Hard to start = resisting going from rest to motion
Hard to stop = resisting going from motion to rest

questionText: When a Minecraft rocket fires downward, it launches upward. Which of Newton's Laws explains this?
answerFormula: Newton's Third Law — for every action (exhaust shooting down) there is an equal and opposite reaction (rocket going up)
distractors: Newton's First Law — the rocket was at rest and stays in motion
Newton's Second Law — force equals mass times acceleration
solutionSteps: The rocket pushes exhaust gases downward (action)
The gases push back on the rocket upward (reaction)
Action-reaction pairs: Newton's Third Law

questionText: A block weighing 40 N rests on a surface with an area of 8 cm². What is the pressure?
solutionSteps: Pressure = Force ÷ Area
Pressure = 40 N ÷ 8 cm²
Pressure = 5 N/cm²

questionText: Why does a person walking on snow with snowshoes sink less than a person walking without snowshoes?
answerFormula: Snowshoes spread the person's weight over a larger area, reducing pressure on the snow
distractors: Snowshoes increase the force pushing down
solutionSteps: Pressure = Force ÷ Area
Same force (weight of person)
Larger area (snowshoes) → smaller pressure
Less pressure = less sinking

questionText: Metal spoon in hot soup gets warm at the handle. This is an example of what type of heat transfer?
answerFormula: Conduction — heat transfers through direct contact along the metal spoon
distractors: Convection — hot liquid circulates to warm the spoon
Radiation — heat rays travel from soup to spoon
solutionSteps: Conduction: heat transfer through direct contact in solids
Convection: heat transfer through fluid movement
Radiation: heat transfer through electromagnetic waves
Spoon touching hot soup: conduction

questionText: When you warm your hands by a campfire without touching it, what type of heat transfer is happening?
answerFormula: Radiation — heat energy travels as electromagnetic waves from the fire to your hands
distractors: Conduction — direct contact between fire and hands
Convection — air currents carry the heat
solutionSteps: You're not touching the fire: not conduction
Heat travels through air as waves: radiation
The sun also warms Earth by radiation

questionText: All gold jewelry is made of the same type of atom. This means gold is...
answerFormula: An element — a pure substance made of only one type of atom
distractors: A compound — made of two atoms bonded together
A mixture — gold mixed with air
solutionSteps: An element = pure substance with only one type of atom
All gold atoms are the same (gold/Au)
Gold is an element

questionText: Water (H₂O) is made of hydrogen atoms and oxygen atoms bonded together. What type of substance is water?
answerFormula: A compound — two or more different elements chemically bonded together
distractors: An element — it has a chemical formula
A mixture — hydrogen and oxygen are mixed
solutionSteps: Compound: two or more different elements chemically bonded
H₂O contains hydrogen (H) and oxygen (O) bonded together
Water is a compound (not just mixed — bonded)

questionText: On the periodic table, the element with symbol 'Fe' has atomic number 26. What does the atomic number tell you?
answerFormula: The number of protons in the nucleus (and electrons in a neutral atom) — 26 protons
distractors: The mass of the atom in grams
The number of neutrons in the nucleus
solutionSteps: Atomic number = number of protons
Fe (iron) has atomic number 26 → 26 protons
A neutral atom also has 26 electrons

questionText: Looking at the periodic table, elements in the same column (group) tend to have similar properties. What group do sodium (Na) and potassium (K) belong to, and what do they have in common?
answerFormula: Group 1 (alkali metals) — both are reactive metals that react vigorously with water
distractors: Group 18 (noble gases) — both are unreactive gases
solutionSteps: Na and K are both in Group 1 (left column)
Group 1 = alkali metals
Common properties: soft, silvery, very reactive with water

questionText: When you bake a cake, the batter turns into something you can't turn back. This is a...
answerFormula: Chemical reaction — a new substance with new properties was formed
distractors: Physical change — just a shape change
Mixture — the ingredients are still separate
solutionSteps: New substance formed (baked cake ≠ raw batter)
The change is irreversible
New properties (texture, color, smell) = chemical reaction

questionText: In a Minecraft furnace, iron ore turns into iron ingots. What evidence shows this is a chemical reaction?
answerFormula: A new substance (iron ingot) with different properties from the ore was formed — this indicates a chemical reaction
distractors: It's a physical change because the iron was just melted
It's a mixture because ore and iron are combined
solutionSteps: Evidence of chemical reaction: new substance formed
Iron ore (reddish rock) → iron ingot (shiny metal)
Different properties = new substance = chemical reaction

questionText: A pattern: Position 1 = 4, Position 2 = 7, Position 3 = 10, Position 4 = 13. Write a formula for Position n.
solutionSteps: Find common difference: 7-4=3, 10-7=3 → multiply n by 3
Formula: 3n + 1

questionText: Minecraft torches needed per level of a dungeon: Level 1 = 5, Level 2 = 8, Level 3 = 11. Write a formula and predict Level 10.
solutionSteps: Common difference: 3 per level
Formula: T = 3L + 2

questionText: In a 3×3 grid, each row and column has shapes circle, square, triangle. Row 1: circle, square, ___. Row 2: square, triangle, circle. Row 3: triangle, ___, square. Fill in the blanks.
answerFormula: Row 1 blank: triangle; Row 3 blank: circle
solutionSteps: Row 1 has circle and square → needs triangle
Row 3 has triangle and square → needs circle
Check columns: Column 2 has square and triangle → needs circle ✓

questionText: A 3×3 number matrix: Row 1: 1, 2, 3. Row 2: 4, 5, 6. Row 3: 7, 8, ___. What is the pattern and missing number?
answerFormula: 9 — numbers increase by 1 left to right, and by 3 top to bottom
solutionSteps: Row 1 increases by 1: 1, 2, 3
Row 3: 7, 8, ? — increase by 1: ? = 9
Each column increases by 3 top to bottom ✓

questionText: Alice is taller than Bob. Bob is taller than Carol. Carol is taller than David. Who is the shortest?
solutionSteps: Alice > Bob > Carol > David
Follow the chain of inequalities
David is at the bottom of the chain → shortest

questionText: A detective has 4 suspects (W, X, Y, Z). Clue 1: W was not in the building. Clue 2: X left before the crime. Clue 3: Y and Z were both there, but only one acted alone. Clue 4: Z has an alibi. Who did it?
solutionSteps: W: eliminated (not in building)
X: eliminated (left before crime)
Z: eliminated (alibi)
Only Y remains → Y did it

questionText: A car travels 150 miles in 3 hours. Is this a proportional relationship? What is the constant of proportionality?
solutionSteps: Proportional: y = kx, where k is the constant
Miles/hours: 150/3 = 50
k = 50 miles per hour (constant rate)

questionText: A Minecraft farm produces crops: 0 days = 0 crops, 1 day = 8 crops, 2 days = 16 crops, 3 days = 24 crops. Is this proportional? What equation represents it?
distractors: No, not proportional because crops increase
solutionSteps: Check: does y/x always equal the same number?
Proportional relationship: y = 8x

questionText: A $60 jacket is on sale for 25% off. What is the sale price?
solutionSteps: Sale price = original - discount
Sale price = $60 - $15 = $45

questionText: A Minecraft village grows from 80 to 100 villagers. What is the percent increase?
solutionSteps: Percent change = (new - old) / old × 100

questionText: Calculate: -3/4 + 1/2
solutionSteps: Find common denominator: 4

questionText: A detective's car goes -2.5 miles (backing up) then 6.75 miles forward. What is the net distance from the start?
solutionSteps: 4.25 miles in the forward direction

questionText: Solve: 2x + 5 = 17
solutionSteps: Subtract 5 from both sides: 2x = 12
Divide both sides by 2: x = 6

questionText: In Minecraft, 3x - 7 = 2x + 4 represents diamond levels. Solve for x.
solutionSteps: Subtract 2x from both sides: x - 7 = 4
Add 7 to both sides: x = 11

questionText: You flip a fair coin. What is the probability of getting heads?
solutionSteps: Total outcomes: 2 (heads or tails)
Favorable outcomes: 1 (heads)
Probability = 1/2 = 0.5 = 50%

questionText: A Minecraft loot chest has 3 swords, 2 bows, and 5 arrows. You randomly pick one item. What is the probability of getting a sword?
solutionSteps: Total items: 3 + 2 + 5 = 10
Favorable outcomes: 3 (swords)
Probability = 3/10 = 30%

questionText: A scale drawing of a playground uses 1 inch = 10 feet. The drawing shows a sandbox that is 3.5 inches wide. How wide is the actual sandbox?
solutionSteps: Scale: 1 inch = 10 feet
Actual width = drawing width × scale factor
3.5 inches × 10 = 35 feet

questionText: Two Minecraft castles are similar figures. Castle A is 24 blocks tall and 16 blocks wide. Castle B is 6 blocks tall. How wide is Castle B?
solutionSteps: Scale factor: 24/6 = 4 (A is 4× taller than B)
Width of B = Width of A ÷ 4
16 ÷ 4 = 4 blocks wide

questionText: A 2 kg ball is thrown up and reaches the top of its path. At the very top, it has...
answerFormula: Maximum potential energy and zero kinetic energy
solutionSteps: At the top, velocity = 0
KE = ½mv²; v = 0 → KE = 0
All energy is potential: PE = mgh (maximum height)

questionText: A 3 kg Minecraft block falls from a height of 5 meters. Calculate its gravitational PE at the top. (g = 10 m/s²)
solutionSteps: PE = mgh
PE = 3 kg × 10 m/s² × 5 m
PE = 150 joules

questionText: A sound wave has a frequency of 440 Hz and a wavelength of 0.77 meters. What is its speed?
answerFormula: Approximately 339 m/s (speed of sound in air)
solutionSteps: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength
v = f × λ

questionText: Two Minecraft note blocks play: one with high amplitude and one with low amplitude. What does amplitude affect?
answerFormula: Amplitude affects loudness (volume) — higher amplitude = louder sound
distractors: Amplitude affects pitch — higher amplitude = higher pitch
Amplitude affects the speed of sound
solutionSteps: Amplitude = height of the wave
Greater amplitude = more energy in the wave
More energy in a sound wave = louder sound (not higher pitch)

questionText: Minecraft's night vision uses 'vision goggles.' In real life, night vision goggles detect which part of the electromagnetic spectrum?
answerFormula: Infrared radiation (heat radiation) — warm objects emit infrared light even in the dark
distractors: Radio waves — they pick up broadcasts
Ultraviolet light — UV is brighter in the dark
solutionSteps: Human eyes detect visible light only
In the dark: no visible light, but warm objects emit infrared
Night vision goggles detect infrared radiation

questionText: Lemon juice has a pH of 2. What does this tell you?
answerFormula: Lemon juice is acidic (pH below 7 = acid)
solutionSteps: pH scale: 0-6 = acidic, 7 = neutral, 8-14 = basic
pH 2 is on the acidic side
Lemon juice is a strong acid

questionText: A chef mixes vinegar (acid, pH 3) with baking soda (base, pH 9). Bubbles form. What type of reaction is this?
answerFormula: A neutralization reaction — acid + base react to form water, salt, and CO₂ gas (the bubbles)
distractors: A physical change — just mixing two liquids
An endothermic reaction absorbing all the heat
solutionSteps: Acid (vinegar) + Base (baking soda) = neutralization
Products: water + salt + carbon dioxide gas
The bubbles are CO₂ — a chemical reaction occurred

questionText: Table salt (NaCl) is formed when sodium (Na) and chlorine (Cl) bond. What type of bond forms when one atom transfers electrons to another?
answerFormula: Ionic bond — sodium gives an electron to chlorine, creating oppositely charged ions that attract each other
distractors: Covalent bond — they share electrons equally
No bond — they just stay near each other
solutionSteps: Ionic bonding: one atom gives electron(s) to another
Na loses 1 electron → Na⁺ (positive ion)
Cl gains 1 electron → Cl⁻ (negative ion)
Opposite charges attract → ionic bond → NaCl

questionText: In a water molecule (H₂O), hydrogen and oxygen share electrons. What type of bond is this?
answerFormula: Covalent bond — atoms share electrons rather than transferring them
distractors: Ionic bond — oxygen takes electrons from hydrogen
Metallic bond — found only in metals
solutionSteps: Covalent bonding: atoms share electron pairs
Oxygen shares electrons with each hydrogen atom
Sharing (not transferring) electrons = covalent bond

questionText: The Fibonacci sequence: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ___. What comes next?
answerFormula: 21 (each term = sum of the two before it)
solutionSteps: Rule: each term = previous two terms added
The next term is 21

questionText: A Minecraft spiral staircase follows a Fibonacci-like pattern: 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21, ___. If the rule is a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2), what is the next term?
solutionSteps: Rule: a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2)

questionText: A Minecraft flat (2D) L-shaped piece is rotated 90° clockwise. If the short arm pointed right and the long arm pointed up, where do they point after rotation?
answerFormula: Short arm points down; long arm points right
distractors: Short arm points up; long arm points left
Short arm points left; long arm points down
solutionSteps: Rotation 90° clockwise: up → right, right → down, down → left, left → up
Short arm (was right) → now points down
Long arm (was up) → now points right

questionText: Three friends — Ava, Ben, Cal — each have one pet: dog, cat, fish. Ava doesn't have the fish. Ben doesn't have the cat. Cal doesn't have the dog. Who has the cat?
solutionSteps: Ben doesn't have cat, Cal doesn't have dog
Cal can have cat or fish; Ben can have dog or fish
If Cal has cat: Ben gets fish, Ava gets dog. But Ava doesn't have fish (ok)
If Cal has fish: Ben has dog, Ava has cat — check: Ava doesn't have fish ✓, Ben doesn't have cat ✓
Both scenarios: Ava = cat

questionText: Four Minecraft players (1, 2, 3, 4) each chose a different biome (forest, desert, ocean, jungle). Player 1 didn't pick desert or jungle. Player 2 picked ocean. Player 3 picked jungle. What did Player 4 pick?
solutionSteps: Player 2: ocean. Player 3: jungle (eliminate these)
Player 1: not desert, not jungle → Player 1 has forest or ocean. Ocean is taken. Player 1 = forest
Remaining: desert → Player 4 = desert

questionText: You pick 4 apples from one tree and 7 apples from another tree. How many apples did you pick in total?
solutionSteps: First tree: 4 apples
Second tree: 7 apples

questionText: A detective found 4 hiding spots. Each spot has 6 clues. How many clues total?
solutionSteps: Hiding spots: 4
Clues per spot: 6
Multiply: 4 × 6 = 24

questionText: You arrange cookies on a baking tray: 5 rows with 4 cookies each. How many cookies fit on the tray?
solutionSteps: Rows: 5
Cookies per row: 4
Multiply: 5 × 4 = 20

questionText: A nature guide finds 18 butterflies and wants to put them equally in 3 jars. How many butterflies per jar?
solutionSteps: Total butterflies: 18
Number of jars: 3
Divide: 18 ÷ 3 = 6

questionText: You bake a cake and cut it into 6 equal slices. You eat 2 slices. What fraction of the cake did you eat?
solutionSteps: Total slices: 6
Slices eaten: 2
Fraction = 2/6 = 1/3

questionText: A detective starts with 15 suspects. After interviewing them, 8 are cleared. How many suspects remain?
solutionSteps: Start: 15 suspects
Cleared: 8
Subtract: 15 - 8 = 7

questionText: A nature explorer counted 529 bird sightings. What digit is in the tens place?
solutionSteps: 529 — hundreds: 5, tens: 2, ones: 9
The tens digit is 2

questionText: Your garden is 5 squares wide and 3 squares long. How many square units is the area?
solutionSteps: Width: 5
Length: 3
Area = 5 × 3 = 15 square units

questionText: A detective draws a rectangular clue board that is 7 cm long and 4 cm wide. What is the perimeter?
solutionSteps: Add all sides: 7 + 4 + 7 + 4 = 22 cm

questionText: A chef decorates cookies in a pattern: icing, sprinkles, icing, sprinkles, icing, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: The pattern alternates: icing, sprinkles
After icing comes sprinkles

questionText: Nature explorer counts bird eggs by 3s: 3, 6, 9, 12, ___. What comes next?
solutionSteps: Counting by 3s

questionText: A detective's shield is shaped like a pentagon. How many sides does a pentagon have?
solutionSteps: Penta means 5 in Greek
A pentagon has 5 sides

questionText: A butterfly wing has exactly 7 spots. If the other wing is a mirror image, how many total spots does the butterfly have?
solutionSteps: One wing: 7 spots
Mirror image: 7 spots
Total: 7 + 7 = 14

questionText: A cookie is cut into 5 equal pieces. You eat one piece. What fraction did you eat?
solutionSteps: Total pieces: 5
You ate: 1 piece
Fraction = 1/5

questionText: A nature chart shows: sparrows=6, robins=9, eagles=3. Which bird appeared most?
solutionSteps: Compare the values: 6, 9, 3
9 is the highest
Robins appeared most

questionText: A cooking class starts at 2:15 PM and lasts 45 minutes. What time does it end?
solutionSteps: Start: 2:15 PM
Add 45 minutes

questionText: A detective collected 347 clues. If you round to the nearest hundred, how many clues is that?
solutionSteps: 347 — look at tens digit: 4
4 < 5, so round down
Rounded to nearest hundred: 300

questionText: A nature scientist tests two bar magnets. She pushes north poles together. What will happen?
solutionSteps: Like poles repel each other
North + North = repel

questionText: A detective notes footprints: circle, square, circle, square, ___. What shape comes next?
solutionSteps: Pattern: circle, square, circle, square...
The pattern repeats every 2
Next is: circle

answerFormula: On the counter behind you (opposite side from the oven)
solutionSteps: The oven light travels in straight lines.
Your body blocks the light from the oven.
The blocked area on the counter behind you is your shadow.

questionText: As a detective, you're examining a room with a single lamp. The suspect stands between the lamp and a wall. Where does the suspect's shadow appear?
answerFormula: On the wall behind the suspect (opposite side from the lamp)
distractors: Between the suspect and the lamp
solutionSteps: The lamp's light travels in straight lines.
The suspect's body blocks the light from the lamp.
The blocked area on the wall behind the suspect is his shadow.

questionText: In Minecraft, a player drops a block of gold and a block of wood into water. The wood floats and the gold sinks. What does this tell you about their densities?
distractors: Gold is less dense than wood
Gold and wood have the same density
solutionSteps: Gold sinks in water, wood floats
Sinking means denser than the liquid below it
Gold is denser than wood

questionText: A chef is baking a cake. She mixes two ingredients, flour and sugar, in a bowl. The sugar sinks and the flour floats. What does this tell you about their densities?
distractors: Flour and sugar have the same density
solutionSteps: Sugar sinks in flour, flour floats
Sinking means denser than the substance below it
Sugar is denser than flour

questionText: A detective finds two bullets at a crime scene. One bullet sinks in a pool of water, the other floats. What does this indicate about their densities?
answerFormula: The sinking bullet is denser than water, the floating bullet is less dense
distractors: Both bullets have the same density
The floating bullet is denser than water
solutionSteps: One bullet sinks, the other floats
Sinking means denser than the liquid below it
The sinking bullet is denser than water, the floating bullet is less dense

questionText: You are building a castle in Minecraft with 24 rooms. Each room has 36 blocks. How many blocks do you need in total?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 24 × 36

questionText: You are baking cookies for a party. The recipe makes 14 cookies and you need to make 28 batches. How many cookies will you have?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 14 × 28

questionText: A detective finds 12 clues at each of 22 crime scenes. How many total clues did the detective find?
solutionSteps: Multiply: 12 × 22

questionText: In Minecraft, you mined 3/8 of a diamond vein before sunset and 2/8 after sunset. What fraction of the vein did you mine in total?
solutionSteps: Same denominators: 8
Add numerators: 3 + 2 = 5
Total: 5/8

questionText: You used 2/5 of a cup of flour and 1/5 of a cup of sugar for a recipe. What's the total amount of ingredients used?
solutionSteps: Same denominators: 5
Add numerators: 2 + 1 = 3
Total: 3/5

questionText: Detective Fox searched 1/4 of the mansion before lunch and 2/4 after lunch. What fraction of the mansion did he search in total?
solutionSteps: Same denominators: 4
Add numerators: 1 + 2 = 3
Total: 3/4

questionText: Steve is building a Minecraft house. The floor plan is two rectangles, one 7x5 blocks, the other 4x3 blocks. What's the total area in square blocks?
solutionSteps: Area of rectangle 1: 7 x 5 = 35 square blocks
Area of rectangle 2: 4 x 3 = 12 square blocks
Total area: 35 + 12 = 47 square blocks

questionText: Mom is baking a big rectangular cake of 10x8 inches, but cuts a square of 3x3 inches for decoration. What's the remaining cake area in square inches?
solutionSteps: Area of big cake: 10 x 8 = 80 square inches
Area of cut-out: 3 x 3 = 9 square inches
Remaining area = 80 - 9 = 77 square inches

questionText: Detective Spot inspects two rooms for clues. Room 1 is 6x5 feet, Room 2 is 4x3 feet. What's the total area he searched in square feet?
solutionSteps: Area of Room 1: 6 x 5 = 30 square feet
Area of Room 2: 4 x 3 = 12 square feet
Total area: 30 + 12 = 42 square feet

questionText: In Minecraft, Steve mines 5 diamonds on day 1, 8 on day 2, 11 on day 3. Write a formula for the diamonds he mines on day n.
solutionSteps: Identify the difference: 8 - 5 = 3, 11 - 8 = 3, so 3n
Substitute n=1 into 3n: 3(1)+? = 5, ? = 2
Thus, formula: D = 3n + 2

questionText: In a kitchen, 3 eggs make 6 pancakes, 4 eggs make 8 pancakes, 5 eggs make 10 pancakes. Find a formula for pancakes (P) for n eggs.
solutionSteps: Notice that double the eggs gives the pancakes, so 2n
Check with n=3: P = 2(3) = 6, correct
Therefore, formula: P = 2n

questionText: In a mystery, for every 2 clues found, 3 suspects are eliminated. If 4 clues found, 6 eliminated, 6 clues found, 9 eliminated. Find a formula for clues (C) to suspects (S).
solutionSteps: Observe that 1.5 times the clues gives the suspects, so 1.5C
Test with C=2: S = 1.5(2) = 3, correct
Hence, formula: S = 1.5C

questionText: In Minecraft, to craft a cake you need 3 buckets of milk, 2 sugar, 1 egg, and 3 wheat. If you have 9 milk, 4 sugar, 3 eggs, and 9 wheat, how many cakes can you craft?
solutionSteps: Each cake needs 3 milk, 2 sugar, 1 egg and 3 wheat.
You have enough for 3 cakes based on milk and wheat.
You also have enough for 3 cakes based on sugar and eggs.

questionText: You are baking cookies. Each batch needs 2 cups of flour. You have 7 cups. After making batches, you find you have 1 cup left. How many batches did you make?
solutionSteps: You started with 7 cups of flour.
You have 1 cup left, so you used 6 cups.
2 cups per batch means you made 3 batches.

questionText: A detective has 3 suspects (A, B, C). Clue 1: A has black hair. Clue 2: The culprit has blonde hair. Clue 3: C has brown hair. Who is the culprit?
solutionSteps: A: eliminated (black hair)
C: eliminated (brown hair)
Only B remains → B is the culprit

questionText: In Minecraft, you are building a 1:8 scale model of a tower. If the real tower is 64 blocks tall, how tall should your model be?
solutionSteps: Scale factor: 64/8 = 8 (Real tower is 8x taller)
Model height = Real height ÷ 8
64 ÷ 8 = 8 blocks tall

questionText: In a detective mystery, a map shows a 1 inch = 100 feet scale. If the secret treasure is marked 2.5 inches away from the starting point, how far is it in real life?
solutionSteps: Scale: 1 inch = 100 feet
Actual distance = Map distance × scale factor
2.5 inches × 100 = 250 feet

questionText: In Minecraft, you drop a block of 2 kg from a height of 3 meters. What is the block's potential energy at the top? (g = 10 m/s²)
solutionSteps: PE = mgh
PE = 2 kg × 10 m/s² × 3 m
PE = 60 joules

questionText: You're baking and you slide a 1 kg tray of cookies into the oven. If the tray's speed is 2 m/s, what is its kinetic energy?
solutionSteps: KE = ½mv²
KE = ½ × 1 kg × (2 m/s)²
KE = 2 joules

questionText: In a detective mystery, a 80 kg stone statue is moved from the ground floor to the 2nd floor (5 m high). What's its potential energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
solutionSteps: PE = mgh
PE = 80 kg × 10 m/s² × 5 m
PE = 4000 joules

questionText: When a chef rings a bell in the kitchen, the sound wave travels at 343 m/s. If the bell's frequency is 250 Hz, what is the wavelength?
solutionSteps: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength
Wavelength = speed / frequency
Wavelength = 343 m/s / 250 Hz = 1.372 m

questionText: A detective uses a flashlight with light wavelength of 500 nm. If light speed is 299,792,458 m/s, what is the frequency?
solutionSteps: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength
Frequency = speed / wavelength
Frequency = 299,792,458 m/s / 500 nm = approximately 6 × 10^14 Hz

questionText: In Minecraft, you rotate a flat (2D) T-shaped block 90° counter-clockwise. If the top of the T pointed up and the stem pointed right, where do they point after rotation?
answerFormula: Top points left; stem points up
distractors: Top points right; stem points down
Top points down; stem points left
solutionSteps: Rotation 90° counter-clockwise: up → left, left → down, down → right, right → up
Top of T (was up) → now points left
Stem of T (was right) → now points up

questionText: You are baking a cake and you have a 3D rectangular block of butter. If you rotate it 90° clockwise along the long axis, which face will be on top?
solutionSteps: Identify the long axis of the block
Rotation 90° clockwise along the long axis
The narrow face of the block will be on top

questionText: As a detective, you found a 3D object at the crime scene and have a cross-section of it. If you rotate the cross-section 180°, what shape will you see?
answerFormula: The same shape, but upside down
solutionSteps: Identify the cross-section of the 3D object
Rotation 180°: up → down, down → up
You will see the same shape, but upside down

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: In Minecraft, Sarah builds 4 small houses and 3 big houses. How many houses did she build in total?
solutionSteps: Count the small houses (4) and big houses (3).
Add 4 and 3 to find the total: 4 + 3.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Liam is baking cookies. He places 6 cookies on one tray and 5 on another. How many cookies does he place on both trays together?
solutionSteps: Count the cookies on each tray: 6 and 5.
Add the two amounts: 6 + 5.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Emma wants to buy 3 apples and 4 oranges. How many pieces of fruit does she buy in total?
solutionSteps: Identify the number of apples (3) and oranges (4).
Add the amounts: 3 + 4.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: In a Minecraft world, you build towers with blocks. If you place 2 blocks for each tower, how many blocks are used for 4 towers?
solutionSteps: Number of blocks per tower = 2
Number of towers = 4
Total blocks = 2 x 4 = 8

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: While baking cookies, you put 5 cookies on each tray. If you place cookies on 3 trays, how many cookies are there in total?
solutionSteps: Cookies per tray = 5
Number of trays = 3
Total cookies = 5 x 3 = 15

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: At the grocery store, you have $10. Each toy costs $2. How many toys can you buy with your $10?
solutionSteps: Cost per toy = $2
Total money = $10
Number of toys = 10 ÷ 2 = 5

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: In Minecraft, Sarah builds 4 houses. Each house has 3 windows. How many windows are there in total?
solutionSteps: Multiply the number of houses by the windows per house: 4 x 3.
Calculate 4 x 3 = 12.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Emma is baking cookies. She makes 5 trays, and each tray has 6 cookies. How many cookies does she bake?
solutionSteps: Multiply the number of trays by cookies per tray: 5 x 6.
Calculate 5 x 6 = 30.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Liam goes shopping and buys 3 packs of stickers. Each pack has 8 stickers. How many stickers does Liam buy in total?
solutionSteps: Multiply the number of packs by stickers per pack: 3 x 8.
Calculate 3 x 8 = 24.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Sara is baking cookies. She places 2 trays with 6 cookies on each tray. How many cookies does she bake in total?
solutionSteps: Count the number of trays: 2.
Count cookies in each tray: 6.
Multiply trays by cookies per tray: 2 × 6 = 12.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Liam is shopping and buys 5 packs of stickers with 3 stickers in each pack. How many stickers does Liam buy in total?
solutionSteps: Number of packs: 5.
Stickers in each pack: 3.
Multiply packs by stickers per pack: 5 × 3 = 15.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: You are building a Minecraft house. The walls are rectangular and each wall is 8 blocks long and 4 blocks high. How many blocks are used in one wall?
solutionSteps: Identify the length and height of the wall (8 and 4).
Multiply the length by the height: 8 * 4 = 32 blocks.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: While baking cookies, you place squares of dough that are each 3 inches on a side on the baking sheet. How many 3-inch square cookies can fit in a row along the 15-inch length of the sheet?
solutionSteps: Determine the length of the baking sheet (15 inches).
Divide the length by the side of each square: 15 ÷ 3 = 5.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: You are shopping for a rectangular picture frame that is 20 inches wide and 15 inches tall. If the frame's border is 2 inches wide all around, what is the total area of the glass inside the frame?
solutionSteps: Calculate the inner dimensions by subtracting twice the border width (2 inches on each side): (20 - 4) and (15 - 4).
Multiply the inner width by the inner height: 16 * 11 = 176.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: In Minecraft, a player builds a row of towers. The pattern is 2 blocks high, then 4 blocks high, then 6 blocks high. If the pattern continues, how high will the 5th tower be?
solutionSteps: Identify the pattern: 2, 4, 6, ...
The pattern increases by 2 each time; so the 4th is 8, and the 5th is 10.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Liam is shopping for candies. He buys 5 candies, then 10 candies, then 15 candies. How many candies will he buy on his 4th shopping trip?
solutionSteps: Identify the pattern: 5, 10, 15, ...
The pattern increases by 5 each time; so the 4th shopping trip is 20 candies.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: At the playground, a child pushes a swing to make it move back and forth. If they push gently, the swing moves slowly. What force is making the swing move?
distractors: friction with the air in newtons (N)
solutionSteps: Identify the force applied by the child on the swing, which is pushing.
Understand that pushing creates a force that causes movement.
The force exerted by the child makes the swing move, measured in N.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: A roller coaster zooms down a hill, speeding up as gravity pulls it downward. Which force is causing the coaster to go faster?
solutionSteps: Recognize gravity as the force pulling the coaster downward.
Determine that gravity increases the coaster's speed as it moves downhill.
Gravity exerts a force measured in N, causing acceleration.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: A spaceship is pushed by thrusters to start moving in space. Which force is responsible for the spaceship’s movement?
solutionSteps: Identify the force generated by the thrusters, called thrust.
Understand that thrust pushes the spaceship forward.
Measure the force in N that causes its movement.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: When you put ice (solid water) in your drink, what happens to the ice as it gets warm in the glass?
solutionSteps: Identify that heat causes solids to change to liquids.
Remember that warming ice makes it melt into water.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: When you boil water in the kitchen, what is the gas that forms and escapes into the air?
solutionSteps: Recall that heating water produces steam, which is water in gas form.
Understand that boiling turns the liquid water into vapor.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: In a science lab, if you heat a glass of liquid mercury (Hg), what state will the mercury be in at very high temperatures?
solutionSteps: Understand that heating a liquid increases its kinetic energy.
Realize that at high temperature, mercury turns into vapor.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: In the kitchen, you mix rice and beans in a bowl. Can you separate them easily later? What are they called together?
solutionSteps: Mixtures are made by combining different materials.
Rice and beans can be separated by picking them apart.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: While cooking pasta, you add salt to water. Is the salt part of the water or a mixture? What do we call such a combination?
solutionSteps: Salt dissolves in water, creating a mixture.
Since the salt can be separated out, it's a mixture, not a chemical reaction.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: In the science lab, two powders are mixed together but can be separated by filtering. What type of mixture is this?
solutionSteps: Filtering separates different materials in a mixture.
A mixture where parts are visible and can be separated is called heterogeneous.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: In the kitchen, you stir sugar into hot tea until it disappears. What is happening to the sugar?
distractors: The sugar turns into a different substance
The sugar floats on the tea surface
solutionSteps: When sugar is stirred into hot tea, it spreads out evenly.
The sugar particles break apart and mix with the water molecules.

subject: CHEMISTRY
questionText: While cooking, you add salt to boiling water for pasta. What does the salt do?
distractors: It stays on top of the water
It reacts and turns into gas
solutionSteps: When salt is added to boiling water, it mixes in and disappears.
This process is called dissolving, where the salt becomes part of the water.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: In Minecraft, you build a wall using 4 blocks. If 2 of these blocks are painted red to make a half of the wall, what fraction of the wall is red?
solutionSteps: Count the total number of blocks: 4.
Identify the red blocks: 2.
Write the fraction: red blocks over total blocks = 2/4.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: While baking, you use 3 cups of flour and need to use one-third of a cup for a frosting recipe. How many thirds of a cup are in 1 cup of flour?
solutionSteps: Divide 1 cup of flour into equal parts of 1/3 cup each.
Number of thirds in 1 cup = 1 ÷ 1/3.
Calculate: 1 ÷ 1/3 = 3.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: At the store, you buy a large chocolate bar divided into 4 equal parts. If you eat 1 part, what fraction of the bar did you eat?
solutionSteps: Count the parts: 4.
Figuratively, eating 1 part out of 4.
Write the fraction: 1/4.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: At the playground, a child slides down a slide. Why does the slide slow the child down at the bottom?
distractors: The slide makes the child go faster
The slide pushes the child upward
solutionSteps: Friction opposes motion and acts between the slide and the child's clothes.
Friction is a force that slows things down when surfaces rub together.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: A roller coaster cart moves along the track and slows down before the next hill. What causes this decrease in speed?
distractors: The coaster pushes against the air to slow down
The track cools the coaster as it moves
solutionSteps: Friction between the coaster wheels and the track resists motion.
Friction is a force that slows things down when surfaces rub together.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: A spaceship lands on the moon with a lot of speed. What slows it down as it touches the ground?
distractors: The moon's gravity pushes the spaceship back up
The spaceship's engine slows down the landing
solutionSteps: Friction between the spaceship's landing gear and the moon's surface resists movement.
Friction is the force that occurs when surfaces rub together and can slow objects down.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: On a playground, a seesaw is balanced with a 10 kg child on one side. How much weight must a second child be to balance the seesaw if they sit at the same distance?
solutionSteps: Recognize that the seesaw is balanced at the same distance from the center, so weights must be equal.
Since one child weighs 10 kg, the other must also be 10 kg to balance the seesaw.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: A roller coaster car with a mass of 500 kg is moving at a certain speed. If the car accelerates, what force (in Newtons) is it experiencing due to its mass and acceleration? (Use F = m × a, with a = 2 m/s²)
solutionSteps: Recall the formula F = m × a.
Substitute m = 500 kg and a = 2 m/s² into the formula: F = 500 kg × 2 m/s².
Calculate F = 1000 N.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: A spaceship is in space far from planets. If a 20 kg object is pushed with a force of 10 N, what is its acceleration? (Use a = F / m)
solutionSteps: Recall the formula a = F / m.
Substitute F = 10 N and m = 20 kg into the formula: a = 10 N / 20 kg.
Calculate a = 0.5 m/s².

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: At the playground, your friend is standing in the sun and casting a shadow. If the sun gets lower in the sky in the late afternoon, what happens to the shadow?
solutionSteps: As the sun lowers, the angle of sunlight becomes more oblique.
This causes objects' shadows to stretch longer on the ground.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: During a roller coaster ride, the sunlight hits the track and creates a shadow. When the coaster is at the highest point of the track, what is true about the shadow?
solutionSteps: At the highest point, the sun is nearly overhead.
This makes shadows shorter because the light hits objects from above at a direct angle.

subject: PHYSICS
questionText: In space, astronauts use the Sun to see objects by the shadows it creates. Why do shadows look sharper in space than they do on Earth?
solutionSteps: Shadows are sharper because there is no atmosphere to scatter sunlight.
Without atmospheric diffusion, shadows have clear edges.

subject: MATHEMATICS
questionText: Alex builds a Minecraft house with walls that are symmetrical. The front wall has 4 red blocks on the left side and 4 on the right side. How many red blocks are there in total on the front wall?
solutionSteps: Identify that the front wall has 4 red blocks on each side.
Since the shape is symmetrical, the total is 4 + 4 = 8.

subject: MATHEMATICS
